reforms.description: The government actively promotes private initiatives through provisions in the tax code, including a tax credit for research and experimentation expenditures, to catalyze the development and diffusion of technology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit has been actively utilized by firms since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: While not a major overhaul, the tax credit represents a significant policy tool to encourage R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: Research and experimentation (R&E) expenditures can be expensed and increases in R&E expenditures are subject to a tax credit of 20 per cent of qualified expenditures over a base amount.

reforms.description: The Manufacturing Extension Partnership (MEP) has expanded its network of technology extension centres to assist SMEs in adopting appropriate manufacturing technologies, enhancing their competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating technology adoption among SMEs, the MEP enhances productivity and innovation in the manufacturing sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The MEP has been operational and expanding its services since its inception.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of the MEP represents a significant effort to improve technology transfer and support for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The Manufacturing Extension Partnership (MEP), which links universities, communities, and government through a nation-wide network of technology extension centres to help SMEs adopt appropriate manufacturing technologies, has expanded from 7 to 44 centres.
